DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF THE INFINITY OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT JAMES GUTIERREZ TO APPEAR FOR HIS DEPOSITION AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT JAMES GUTIERREZ SHALL APPEAR FOR HIS DEPOSITION ON OR BEFORE DECEMBER 31, 2023. DEFENDANT JAMES GUTIERREZ SHALL ALSO PAY A MONETARY SANCTION IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,103.25 TO PLAINTIFF, THROUGH PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL OF RECORD, ON OR BEFORE DECEMBER 31, 2023. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM WILLIAM LYNN AT WILLIAMCLYNN@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: WILLIAM LYNN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/JPT)

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Argued. The Court announces the tentative rulings. Mr. James Gutierrez contests the tentative ruling and informs the court he is in compliance except for the deposition. Mr. Nichter responds and announces the Defendant has been served with necessary documents. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF THE INFINITY OWNERS ASSOCIATION's, A CONDOMINIUM's Motion For Discovery Terminating Sanctions Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc., 2023.030.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.*** Plaintiff's motion for discovery terminating sanctions is granted. Defendant's answer to Plaintiff's complaint is hereby stricken, and the clerk will enter defendant's default on the complaint. The Court will sign an order.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Austin Nichter; Feldsott, Lee & Nichter; 949-729-8002; appearing as counsel for plaintiff/cross-defendant The Infinity Owners Association.

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. The Notice of Motion does not specify what the summary judgment is being sought on (complaint, cross-complaint or both), the Separate Statement does not contain facts that purport to support a summary judgment on the complaint. Motion for Summary Adjudication on Affirmative defenses and the cross-complaint is GRANTED. No opposition filed.

Counsel to prepare order. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Dec-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Austin Nichter,Esq., FELDSOTT, LEE & NICHTER (949) 729-8002 Attorneys for Plaintiff THE INFINITY OWNERS ASSOCIATION
No other apperances.

Plaintiff stipulates to Judge Pro Tem Willim Lynn deciding today's motion. All parties give authorization for the clerk to sign the Judge Pro Tem Stipulation on their own behaves.

Court confirms that this matter is unopposed, not contested. Not Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF THE INFINITY OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Motion For Orders Compelling Defendant James Gutierrez To Appear For His Deposition, For Monetary Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 2023.030(A) And 2025.450(G)(1), In The Sum Of $2,103.25

Plaintiff The Infinity Owners Association's unopposed motion to compel Defendant James Gutierrez to appear for his deposition and for monetary sanctions is granted. Defendant James Gutierrez shall appear for his deposition on or before December 31, 2023. Defendant James Gutierrez shall also pay a monetary sanction in the amount of $2,103.25 to Plaintiff, through Plaintiff's counsel of record, on or before December 31, 2023.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem William Lynn at williamclynn@gmail.com.
